def_en: to take in a liquid, gas, or other substance (absorb water, absorb heat); to interest someone completely (absorbed in a book)
def_zh: 吸收（水分，热量）；使全神贯注（沉浸书中）
quote_en: A paper towel can absorb a spill quickly.
quote_zh: 纸巾可以快速吸收洒出的液体。
ex_en: In geography class, we learned that dark roofs absorb more heat than white ones, which affects urban temperatures.
ex_zh: 在地理课上，我们学到深色屋顶比白色屋顶吸收更多热量，这会影响城市温度。

def_en: to give someone a recommendation or suggestion about what to do (advise against, strongly advise)
def_zh: 建议；劝告（劝诫，强烈建议）
quote_en: I advise you to check the weather before going hiking.
quote_zh: 我建议你在去徒步前查看天气。
ex_en: In ancient Greece, citizens would often advise their leaders before a vote in the assembly.
ex_zh: 在古希腊，公民常在公民大会投票前向领袖提出建议。

def_en: a person who is trained or skilled in sports, exercise, or physical activity (professional athlete, Olympic athlete)
def_zh: 运动员（职业运动员，奥林匹克运动员）
quote_en: The young athlete trained every day to improve her running time.
quote_zh: 那位年轻运动员每天训练以提高她的跑步成绩。
ex_en: During the first Olympic Games in 776 BCE, only male athletes competed in a single footrace.
ex_zh: 在公元前776年的第一届奥运会上，只有男性运动员参加了一项赛跑。

def_en: the most important city of a country or region, where the government is based (capital city, national capital); wealth or assets owned by a person or company (financial capital, capital investment)
def_zh: 首都（首都城市，国家首都）；资本（金融资本，资本投资）
quote_en: London is the capital of England and a major financial centre.
quote_zh: 伦敦是英格兰的首都，也是主要的金融中心。
ex_en: The capital of the Inca Empire was Cusco, a city built high in the Andes mountains.
ex_zh: 印加帝国的首都库斯科是一座建在安第斯山脉高处的城市。

def_en: an event or situation where people try to win something or do better than others (fierce competition, enter a competition)
def_zh: 比赛；竞争（激烈竞争，参加比赛）
quote_en: There was healthy competition between the two science fair teams.
quote_zh: 两个科学展览团队之间有着良性的竞争。
ex_en: In medieval Europe, knights took part in jousting competitions to prove their bravery.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的欧洲，骑士通过参加长矛比武竞赛来证明自己的勇气。

def_en: a system of government where citizens vote to make political decisions (direct democracy, representative democracy)
def_zh: 民主（直接民主，代议制民主）
quote_en: In a democracy, every citizen over 18 has the right to vote.
quote_zh: 在民主制度下，每位年满18岁的公民都有投票权。
ex_en: Ancient Athens is often called the birthplace of democracy because citizens could vote on laws.
ex_zh: 古雅典常被称为民主的发源地，因为公民可以对法律进行投票。

def_en: one event or part of a series, especially a TV or radio programme (first episode, final episode); an event or period that is part of a larger sequence (sad episode, brief episode)
def_zh: 一集（第一集，最后一集）；一段经历（伤心的一段，短暂的经历）
quote_en: The season finale episode had the highest ratings of the year.
quote_zh: 季终集获得了全年最高的收视率。
ex_en: One famous episode in Roman history is when Julius Caesar crossed the Rubicon River in 49 BCE.
ex_zh: 罗马历史上著名的一段经历是公元前49年尤利乌斯·恺撒渡过卢比孔河。

def_en: to give something and receive something else in return (exchange money, exchange ideas); a short conversation or argument (heated exchange, friendly exchange)
def_zh: 交换（换钱，交流想法）；短暂的交谈或争论（激烈争论，友好交流）
quote_en: Students can exchange their extra lunch items with friends.
quote_zh: 学生们可以用多余的午餐物品与朋友交换。
ex_en: The Silk Road was a network of routes where people exchanged silk, spices, and ideas between Asia and Europe.
ex_zh: 丝绸之路是一张路线网络，人们在亚洲和欧洲之间交换丝绸、香料和思想。

def_en: the set of rules that explain how words are used in a language (English grammar, correct grammar)
def_zh: 语法（英语语法，正确语法）
quote_en: Learning grammar helps you write clearer sentences.
quote_zh: 学习语法有助于写出更清晰的句子。
ex_en: In 1586, William Bullokar wrote the first English grammar book to help people learn the language.
ex_zh: 1586年，威廉·布洛卡写了第一本英语语法书来帮助人们学习英语。

def_en: a place where sick or injured people receive medical treatment (local hospital, teaching hospital)
def_zh: 医院（本地医院，教学医院）
quote_en: My cousin broke his arm and had to stay in hospital for one night.
quote_zh: 我表哥摔断了胳膊，不得不在医院住了一晚。
ex_en: Florence Nightingale improved hospital conditions during the Crimean War, saving many soldiers’ lives.
ex_zh: 弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔在克里米亚战争期间改善了医院条件，挽救了许多士兵的生命。

def_en: the power to affect someone or something without direct force (strong influence, under the influence); to affect or change something (influence decision, influence behaviour)
def_zh: 影响（强烈影响，受影响）；影响（影响决定，影响行为）
quote_en: The weather can influence a person’s mood.
quote_zh: 天气可以影响人的情绪。
ex_en: The discovery of fire greatly influenced how early humans cooked, stayed warm, and protected themselves.
ex_zh: 火的发现极大地影响了早期人类烹饪、保暖和自我保护的方式。

def_en: a group of people working together for a purpose (charitable organization, non-profit organization); the act of arranging things in an orderly way (good organization, poor organization)
def_zh: 组织（慈善组织，非营利组织）；条理（良好的条理，糟糕的条理）
quote_en: Our school’s student organization plans the annual talent show.
quote_zh: 我们学校的学生组织策划年度才艺表演。
ex_en: The United Nations is an international organization founded in 1945 to keep peace and cooperation among countries.
ex_zh: 联合国是一个成立于1945年的国际组织，旨在维护国家间的和平与合作。

def_en: a fixed, step‑by‑step way of doing something (safety procedure, correct procedure)
def_zh: 程序；步骤（安全程序，正确步骤）
quote_en: The lab procedure requires wearing goggles and gloves at all times.
quote_zh: 实验室步骤要求始终佩戴护目镜和手套。
ex_en: In a courtroom, the judge follows a strict procedure to ensure a fair trial for everyone.
ex_zh: 在法庭上，法官遵循严格的程序以确保每个人得到公正审判。

def_en: a special set of clothes worn by members of the same group (school uniform, military uniform)
def_zh: 制服（校服，军服）
quote_en: Many students like wearing a uniform because it saves time in the morning.
quote_zh: 许多学生喜欢穿校服，因为早上可以节省时间。
ex_en: Roman soldiers wore a standard uniform – a tunic, armour, and sandals – to show they belonged to the same army.
ex_zh: 罗马士兵穿着标准制服——短袍、盔甲和凉鞋——以表明他们属于同一支军队。

def_en: an object used to fight or attack someone (deadly weapon, nuclear weapon)
def_zh: 武器（致命武器，核武器）
quote_en: A sword is an ancient weapon that required great skill to use.
quote_zh: 剑是一种需要高超技巧才能使用的古代武器。
ex_en: During the Hundred Years’ War, the longbow became a powerful English weapon that changed battlefield tactics.
ex_zh: 在百年战争期间，长弓成了一种强大的英国武器，改变了战场战术。

def_en: to leave someone or something behind permanently (abandon ship, abandon a project); to stop doing or supporting something (abandon a habit, abandon an idea)
def_zh: 抛弃（弃船，放弃项目）；放弃（戒掉习惯，放弃想法）
quote_en: The crew had to abandon the ship when it started sinking.
quote_zh: 船开始下沉时，船员不得不弃船。
ex_en: In 1587, the English colonists abandoned Roanoke Island for unknown reasons, leaving behind only the word "Croatoan".
ex_zh: 1587年，英国殖民者因不明原因放弃了罗阿诺克岛，只留下了“Croatoan”这个词。

def_en: the state of being away from a place or person (absence from school, long absence); the lack of something (absence of evidence, complete absence)
def_zh: 缺席（缺课，长期缺席）；缺乏（缺乏证据，完全缺乏）
quote_en: His absence from practice meant he couldn't play in the game.
quote_zh: 他缺席训练意味着他不能参加比赛。
ex_en: During the Ice Age, the absence of warm sunlight caused large areas to remain frozen for thousands of years.
ex_zh: 冰河时期，缺乏温暖的阳光导致大片地区冻结了数千年。

def_en: a ring‑shaped bread roll that is boiled then baked (sesame bagel, cream cheese bagel)
def_zh: 贝果（芝麻贝果，奶油芝士贝果）
quote_en: For breakfast, I had a toasted bagel with butter.
quote_zh: 早餐我吃了一个涂黄油的烤贝果。
ex_en: In the 1880s, Jewish immigrants from Poland brought bagels to New York City, where they became a popular street food.
ex_zh: 十九世纪八十年代，来自波兰的犹太移民将贝果带到纽约市，它在那里成了一种受欢迎的街头食品。

def_en: a group of people or things with similar features (main category, age category)
def_zh: 类别（主要类别，年龄组）
quote_en: Please put the books back in the correct category on the shelf.
quote_zh: 请把书放回书架上正确的类别里。
ex_en: Carl Linnaeus, a Swedish scientist, created a system to classify every living thing into categories like kingdom, class, and species.
ex_zh: 瑞典科学家卡尔·林奈创建了一个将每种生物分类到界、纲、种等类别的系统。

def_en: a person or animal that you spend time with (faithful companion, travel companion)
def_zh: 同伴（忠实伙伴，旅行同伴）
quote_en: My dog has been my loyal companion for five years.
quote_zh: 我的狗已经做了我五年忠实的伙伴。
ex_en: In The Adventures of Tom Sawyer, Huckleberry Finn is Tom’s closest companion during their mischief on the Mississippi.
ex_zh: 在《汤姆·索亚历险记》中，哈克贝利·费恩是汤姆在密西西比河上恶作剧时最亲密的伙伴。

def_en: a straight line from one side of a circle to the other, passing through the centre (measure the diameter, 10 cm in diameter)
def_zh: 直径（测量直径，直径10厘米）
quote_en: The pizza had a diameter of 30 centimetres, enough for four people.
quote_zh: 那个披萨直径30厘米，足够四个人吃。
ex_en: The Earth's diameter is about 12,742 kilometres, which is almost four times the diameter of the Moon.
ex_zh: 地球直径约12,742公里，几乎是月球直径的四倍。

def_en: a sudden explosion of steam and lava from a volcano (volcanic eruption, violent eruption); a sudden start of something angry or violent (eruption of anger, eruption of fighting)
def_zh: 火山喷发（火山喷发，猛烈喷发）；爆发（怒气爆发，打斗爆发）
quote_en: The eruption sent ash clouds high into the atmosphere.
quote_zh: 火山喷发将火山灰云送入高空。
ex_en: In AD 79, the eruption of Mount Vesuvius buried the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under metres of ash.
ex_zh: 公元79年，维苏威火山的喷发将罗马城市庞贝和赫库兰尼姆埋在数米厚的火山灰下。

def_en: to live in a particular place (inhabit an island, inhabit a region)
def_zh: 居住于（居住于岛屿，居住于地区）
quote_en: Many birds inhabit the wetlands during the spring migration.
quote_zh: 许多鸟类在春季迁徙期间栖息于湿地。
ex_en: Before European explorers arrived, millions of bison inhabited the Great Plains of North America.
ex_zh: 在欧洲探险家到来之前，数百万头野牛栖息于北美大平原。

def_en: a person who teaches a practical skill or sport (driving instructor, fitness instructor)
def_zh: 教练；指导者（驾驶教练，健身教练）
quote_en: The swimming instructor taught us how to float on our backs.
quote_zh: 游泳教练教我们如何仰面漂浮。
ex_en: In ancient Greece, a paidotribes was a gymnastics instructor who trained young boys in wrestling, running, and jumping.
ex_zh: 在古希腊，体育老师是一种体操教练，训练年轻男孩摔跤、跑步和跳跃。

def_en: something that stops an activity or conversation for a short time (frequent interruption, rude interruption)
def_zh: 打断；中断（频繁打断，粗鲁打断）
quote_en: The fire alarm caused a brief interruption to our maths lesson.
quote_zh: 火警导致我们的数学课短暂中断。
ex_en: During the Constitutional Convention in 1787, a sudden thunderstorm caused an interruption, and Benjamin Franklin used the moment to make a speech.
ex_zh: 1787年制宪会议期间，一场突如其来的雷暴造成了中断，本杰明·富兰克林借此机会发表了演讲。

def_en: a room or building where scientific experiments are done (science laboratory, research laboratory)
def_zh: 实验室（科学实验室，研究实验室）
quote_en: We mixed chemicals carefully in the school laboratory.
quote_zh: 我们在学校实验室小心地混合化学品。
ex_en: Marie Curie worked in a small, poorly equipped laboratory, yet she discovered two new elements: polonium and radium.
ex_zh: 玛丽·居里在一间狭小、设备简陋的实验室里工作，却发现了两种新元素：钋和镭。

def_en: the number of miles a vehicle has travelled (high mileage, low mileage); the number of miles a vehicle can travel on a certain amount of fuel (good mileage, poor mileage)
def_zh: 里程（高里程，低里程）；燃油效率（省油，费油）
quote_en: This used car has low mileage and runs very smoothly.
quote_zh: 这辆二手车里程很低，运行非常平稳。
ex_en: During the 1908 New York to Paris car race, the winning car covered over 22,000 miles – a remarkable mileage for its time.
ex_zh: 在1908年纽约到巴黎的汽车比赛中，获胜的汽车行驶了超过22,000英里——这在当时是一个惊人的里程。

def_en: a person who plays a musical instrument or sings (professional musician, talented musician)
def_zh: 音乐家（职业音乐家，有天赋的音乐家）
quote_en: The young musician practised the violin for two hours every day.
quote_zh: 那位年轻的音乐家每天练习小提琴两小时。
ex_en: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart was a child musician who could play the piano and compose music by the age of five.
ex_zh: 沃尔夫冈·阿马德乌斯·莫扎特是一位儿童音乐家，五岁时就能弹钢琴并作曲。

def_en: to discuss something formally to reach an agreement (negotiate a deal, negotiate a contract)
def_zh: 谈判（谈判达成交易，谈判合同）
quote_en: The two countries met to negotiate a peace treaty.
quote_zh: 两国会面谈判和平条约。
ex_en: In 1215, English nobles forced King John to negotiate the Magna Carta, which limited the king's power.
ex_zh: 1215年，英国贵族迫使约翰国王谈判签署《大宪章》，限制了国王的权力。

def_en: a person who betrays their country, group, or friend (traitor to the crown, turn traitor)
def_zh: 叛徒（背叛王冠的叛徒，成为叛徒）
quote_en: Benedict Arnold is remembered as a traitor in American history.
quote_zh: 本尼迪克特·阿诺德在美国历史上被记住为叛徒。
ex_en: During the American Revolution, Benedict Arnold secretly agreed to hand over a fort to the British, becoming a famous traitor.
ex_zh: 美国独立战争期间，本尼迪克特·阿诺德秘密同意将一座堡垒交给英军，成为一个著名的叛徒。

def_en: correct in all details; exact (accurate measurement, accurate information)
def_zh: 准确的（精确测量，准确信息）
quote_en: The weather forecast was accurate – it rained at exactly 3 pm.
quote_zh: 天气预报很准确——下午三点整下雨了。
ex_en: Ancient Egyptian architects built the pyramids with such accurate alignment that the sides face exactly north, south, east, and west.
ex_zh: 古埃及建筑师建造的金字塔方向如此精确，各面正好朝北、南、东、西。

def_en: every other or second (alternate days, alternate rows); available as another choice (alternate route, alternate plan)
def_zh: 交替的（每隔一天，隔行）；备用的（备用路线，备用计划）
quote_en: We take turns cleaning the board on alternate days.
quote_zh: 我们每隔一天轮流擦黑板。
ex_en: In the 1840s, the Oregon Trail had an alternate route called the Sublette Cutoff that saved travellers about 70 miles.
ex_zh: 十九世纪四十年代，俄勒冈小径有一条名为“萨布莱特捷径”的备用路线，为旅行者节省了约70英里。

def_en: the power to give orders or make decisions (legal authority, local authority); an expert whose opinion is respected (leading authority, trusted authority)
def_zh: 权威（法律权威，地方当局）；权威人士（领先权威，可信权威）
quote_en: The teacher has the authority to set classroom rules.
quote_zh: 老师有权制定课堂规则。
ex_en: The ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle was considered a leading authority on biology and physics for nearly 2,000 years.
ex_zh: 古希腊哲学家亚里士多德在近2000年里被认为是生物学和物理学领域的权威。

def_en: the qualities that make a person or place different (strong character, moral character); a person in a story or play (main character, fictional character)
def_zh: 性格（坚强性格，道德品格）；角色（主要角色，虚构角色）
quote_en: Honesty is an important part of good character.
quote_zh: 诚实是良好品格的重要部分。
ex_en: In The Adventures of Tom Sawyer, the character of Becky Thatcher is Tom's new classmate and love interest.
ex_zh: 在《汤姆·索亚历险记》中，贝基·撒切尔这个角色是汤姆的新同学和心仪对象。

def_en: consisting of many connected parts; difficult to understand (complex problem, complex system)
def_zh: 复杂的（复杂问题，复杂系统）
quote_en: The human brain is a complex organ with billions of nerve cells.
quote_zh: 人脑是一个复杂的器官，拥有数十亿神经细胞。
ex_en: The ancient Roman aqueducts were a complex system of channels and bridges that brought fresh water to cities.
ex_zh: 古罗马的引水渠是一个由水道和桥梁组成的复杂系统，为城市带来淡水。

def_en: to give all your attention to one thing (concentrate hard, concentrate on work)
def_zh: 集中注意力（努力集中，专注于工作）
quote_en: Please concentrate on your test and ignore the noise outside.
quote_zh: 请集中注意力考试，不要理会外面的噪音。
ex_en: Thomas Edison could concentrate for hours in his laboratory, which helped him invent the light bulb after thousands of attempts.
ex_zh: 托马斯·爱迪生能在实验室里集中注意力数小时，这帮助他在数千次尝试后发明了电灯泡。

def_en: an area surrounded by a fence or wall (livestock enclosure, outdoor enclosure)
def_zh: 围场（牲畜围场，室外围场）
quote_en: The zoo built a new enclosure for the pandas with a small pond.
quote_zh: 动物园为大熊猫建了一个带小池塘的新围场。
ex_en: In medieval England, wealthy landowners built stone enclosures called castles to protect themselves and their animals.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的英格兰，富裕的地主建造了名为城堡的石制围场来保护自己和牲畜。

def_en: relating to managing a company or organization (executive decision, executive officer); a senior manager (company executive, chief executive)
def_zh: 行政的（行政决策，执行官）；高管（公司高管，首席执行官）
quote_en: The executive board meets every month to review the budget.
quote_zh: 执行董事会每月开会审查预算。
ex_en: The executive branch of the US government, led by the President, carries out and enforces laws.
ex_zh: 美国政府的行政部门由总统领导，负责执行和实施法律。

def_en: happening slowly over a period of time (gradual change, gradual improvement)
def_zh: 逐渐的（逐渐变化，逐渐改善）
quote_en: There has been a gradual increase in the temperature over the past week.
quote_zh: 过去一周气温逐渐升高。
ex_en: The Grand Canyon was formed by the gradual erosion of the Colorado River over millions of years.
ex_zh: 大峡谷是由科罗拉多河数百万年的逐渐侵蚀形成的。

def_en: exactly the same in every detail (identical twins, identical copies)
def_zh: 完全相同的（同卵双胞胎，一模一样的副本）
quote_en: The two snowflakes looked nearly identical under the microscope.
quote_zh: 两片雪花在显微镜下看起来几乎一模一样。
ex_en: Identical twins share 100% of their DNA, which is why they look so similar.
ex_zh: 同卵双胞胎共享100%的DNA，这就是为什么他们看起来如此相似。

def_en: against the law (illegal activity, illegal parking)
def_zh: 非法的（非法活动，违章停车）
quote_en: It is illegal to copy a copyrighted book without permission.
quote_zh: 未经许可复制受版权保护的书籍是违法的。
ex_en: In 1919, the 18th Amendment made it illegal to make or sell alcohol in the United States, leading to the Prohibition era.
ex_zh: 1919年，第十八修正案规定在美国制造或销售酒精是非法的，导致了禁酒时代。

def_en: the state of not being guilty of a crime (prove innocence, maintain innocence); lack of experience or knowledge of evil (childhood innocence, lost innocence)
def_zh: 清白（证明清白，坚持清白）；天真（童年天真，失去纯真）
quote_en: The evidence proved his innocence after he was wrongly accused.
quote_zh: 证据证明了他被错误指控后的清白。
ex_en: In To Kill a Mockingbird (public domain in some countries), Scout's innocence makes her question the unfairness of her town.
ex_zh: 在《杀死一只知更鸟》中（在一些国家属于公有领域），斯考特的天真使她质疑镇上的不公。

def_en: the act of controlling and organizing something (time management, waste management); the people who run a company (senior management, middle management)
def_zh: 管理（时间管理，废物管理）；管理层（高级管理层，中层管理层）
quote_en: Good management of study time leads to better grades.
quote_zh: 良好的学习时间管理能带来更好的成绩。
ex_en: Frederick Taylor, known as the father of scientific management, studied how factories could work more efficiently in the early 1900s.
ex_zh: 弗雷德里克·泰勒被称为科学管理之父，他在二十世纪初研究了工厂如何更高效地运作。

def_en: a particular time or event (special occasion, formal occasion); a suitable time or opportunity (on occasion, rare occasion)
def_zh: 场合（特殊场合，正式场合）；时机（偶尔，罕见时机）
quote_en: Graduation is a happy occasion for the whole family.
quote_zh: 毕业典礼是全家人高兴的场合。
ex_en: The first modern Olympic Games in 1896 was a historic occasion that brought together 14 nations in Athens.
ex_zh: 1896年的第一届现代奥运会是一个历史性的场合，将14个国家聚集在雅典。

def_en: decayed and smelling bad (rotten fruit, rotten wood); very bad or unpleasant (rotten weather, rotten luck)
def_zh: 腐烂的（腐烂水果，朽木）；极差的（糟糕天气，倒霉）
quote_en: The apple was rotten inside, so we threw it away.
quote_zh: 那个苹果里面烂了，我们把它扔掉了。
ex_en: In medieval times, people believed that rotten food caused diseases, but we now know that germs are the real cause.
ex_zh: 在中世纪，人们认为腐烂的食物会引起疾病，但现在我们知道细菌才是真正的原因。

def_en: able to be reached or entered (accessible building, easily accessible); easy to understand or use (user‑accessible, accessible language)
def_zh: 可到达的（无障碍建筑，容易到达）；易理解的（用户可访问，易懂语言）
quote_en: The museum is fully accessible for wheelchairs.
quote_zh: 博物馆对轮椅使用者完全无障碍。
ex_en: The Rosetta Stone made ancient Egyptian writing accessible to modern scholars because it had the same text in three scripts.
ex_zh: 罗塞塔石碑使现代学者能够理解古埃及文字，因为它用三种文字刻了相同的内容。

def_en: able to be changed or moved to fit different needs (adjustable seat, adjustable strap)
def_zh: 可调节的（可调节座椅，可调节带子）
quote_en: The bicycle seat is adjustable, so both my brother and I can ride it.
quote_zh: 这个自行车座是可调节的，所以我和我弟弟都能骑。
ex_en: In the 19th century, adjustable spanners were invented, allowing workers to tighten nuts of different sizes with one tool.
ex_zh: 十九世纪，活动扳手被发明出来，让工人可以用一种工具拧紧不同尺寸的螺母。

def_en: to recognise the value of something or be grateful for it (appreciate help, appreciate art); to understand a situation fully (appreciate the problem, appreciate the difference)
def_zh: 欣赏；感激（感激帮助，欣赏艺术）；理解（理解问题，理解差异）
quote_en: I really appreciate you helping me with my homework.
quote_zh: 我非常感激你帮我做作业。
ex_en: In ancient Rome, wealthy citizens appreciated Greek art so much that they copied Greek statues and buildings.
ex_zh: 在古罗马，富裕的公民非常欣赏希腊艺术，以至于他们复制了希腊的雕塑和建筑。

def_en: a person applying for a job or competing in an election (job candidate, presidential candidate)
def_zh: 候选人（求职者，总统候选人）
quote_en: The candidate for class president gave a persuasive speech.
quote_zh: 班长候选人在竞选演讲中很有说服力。
ex_en: In the 1860 US election, Abraham Lincoln was the Republican candidate; he won despite not being on the ballot in ten southern states.
ex_zh: 在1860年的美国大选中，亚伯拉罕·林肯是共和党候选人；尽管他在十个南方州都不在选票上，他仍然获胜了。

def_en: to bring a society to a more advanced stage of development (civilize a nation, civilize a tribe)
def_zh: 使文明（使国家文明，使部落文明）
quote_en: The Romans built roads and aqueducts to civilize the regions they conquered.
quote_zh: 罗马人修建道路和引水渠，使他们征服的地区文明化。
ex_en: The ancient Sumerians are often credited with being the first to civilize by inventing writing, the wheel, and city governments.
ex_zh: 古代苏美尔人常被认为是最早开创文明的人，他们发明了文字、轮子和城市政府。

def_en: a polite expression of praise (nice compliment, pay a compliment); to praise someone (compliment someone's work)
def_zh: 赞美（好的赞美，赞扬）；赞美（称赞某人的工作）
quote_en: He received a compliment on his neat handwriting.
quote_zh: 他因为字迹工整而受到称赞。
ex_en: In ancient China, scholars would compliment each other's calligraphy, as beautiful writing was a sign of education.
ex_zh: 在中国古代，学者们会互相赞美彼此的书法，因为漂亮的字迹是受过教育的标志。

def_en: very bad or shameful (disgraceful behaviour, disgraceful act)
def_zh: 可耻的（可耻行为，可耻举动）
quote_en: Cheating on the test was a disgraceful thing to do.
quote_zh: 在考试中作弊是一件可耻的事情。
ex_en: In the 19th century, many people found it disgraceful that children as young as five worked in coal mines for 12 hours a day.
ex_zh: 十九世纪，许多人觉得五岁儿童每天在煤矿工作12小时是可耻的。

def_en: to make someone feel awkward, shy, or ashamed (embarrass someone, feel embarrassed)
def_zh: 使尴尬（使人尴尬，感到尴尬）
quote_en: I accidentally called my teacher "Mum" and it embarrassed me.
quote_zh: 我不小心把老师叫成了“妈妈”，这让我很尴尬。
ex_en: In 1492, Christopher Columbus's crew embarrassed him when they nearly mutinied because they thought they would never reach land.
ex_zh: 1492年，克里斯托弗·哥伦布的船员几乎叛变，因为他们认为永远到不了陆地，这让他很尴尬。

def_en: the ability to continue doing something difficult for a long time (physical endurance, mental endurance)
def_zh: 忍耐力（身体耐力，精神耐力）
quote_en: Running a marathon requires great endurance and training.
quote_zh: 跑马拉松需要极大的耐力和训练。
ex_en: In 490 BCE, a Greek soldier named Pheidippides showed legendary endurance by running from Marathon to Athens (about 42 km) to announce victory.
ex_zh: 公元前490年，一位名叫菲迪皮茨的希腊士兵展现了传奇般的耐力，从马拉松跑到雅典（约42公里）宣布胜利。

def_en: containing a lot of moisture in the air (hot and humid, humid climate)
def_zh: 潮湿的（炎热潮湿，潮湿气候）
quote_en: The air is so humid today that my glasses keep fogging up.
quote_zh: 今天空气太潮湿了，我的眼镜一直起雾。
ex_en: The Amazon rainforest is one of the most humid places on Earth, with some areas receiving over 3 metres of rain per year.
ex_zh: 亚马逊雨林是地球上最潮湿的地方之一，有些地区年降雨量超过3米。

def_en: very difficult to believe; amazing (incredible story, incredible view)
def_zh: 难以置信的（难以置信的故事，令人惊叹的景色）
quote_en: The magician performed an incredible trick that left everyone speechless.
quote_zh: 魔术师表演了一个让所有人目瞪口呆的惊人魔术。
ex_en: The Great Pyramid of Giza was built with such incredible precision that the gap between stones is thinner than a sheet of paper.
ex_zh: 吉萨大金字塔的建造精度惊人，石头之间的缝隙比一张纸还薄。

def_en: the ability to make sensible decisions (good judgment, poor judgment); an opinion or decision (final judgment, snap judgment)
def_zh: 判断力（良好判断力，糟糕判断力）；判断；意见（最终判决，草率判断）
quote_en: Using good judgment, she waited for an adult before crossing the busy road.
quote_zh: 她运用良好的判断力，等了一个成年人再穿过繁忙的马路。
ex_en: In the story of King Solomon, his wise judgment to offer to split a baby in half revealed the true mother.
ex_zh: 在所罗门王的故事中，他提出将婴儿劈成两半的明智判断揭示了真正的母亲。

def_en: a container with a lid and spout, used for boiling water (electric kettle, whistling kettle)
def_zh: 水壶（电水壶，鸣笛水壶）
quote_en: Mum put the kettle on to make tea after dinner.
quote_zh: 妈妈晚饭后烧水壶泡茶。
ex_en: The first electric kettle was invented in 1891 by the Carpenter Electric Company in the United States.
ex_zh: 第一只电水壶于1891年由美国卡彭特电气公司发明。

def_en: usually; in normal circumstances (ordinarily happen, ordinarily behave)
def_zh: 通常地（通常发生，通常表现）
quote_en: Ordinarily, I walk to school, but today my dad gave me a lift.
quote_zh: 通常我走路上学，但今天爸爸开车送了我。
ex_en: Ordinarily, the Nile River would flood every summer, which made the soil rich for farming in ancient Egypt.
ex_zh: 通常，尼罗河每年夏天会泛滥，这使古埃及的土壤变得肥沃。

def_en: in a way that shows good manners (speak politely, politely ask)
def_zh: 礼貌地（有礼貌地说话，礼貌地询问）
quote_en: The student politely held the door open for the teacher.
quote_zh: 那个学生礼貌地为老师扶着门。
ex_en: In the 18th century, it was considered correct to politely bow or curtsy when greeting someone of higher social rank.
ex_zh: 十八世纪，在问候社会地位较高的人时，礼貌地鞠躬或行屈膝礼被认为是正确的。

def_en: permission to enter a place (gain admittance, refuse admittance)
def_zh: 进入许可（获准进入，拒绝进入）
quote_en: Only ticket holders will be granted admittance to the concert.
quote_zh: 只有持票者才能获准进入音乐会。
ex_en: In 1773, colonists were denied admittance to ships carrying tea, so they disguised themselves and threw the tea into Boston Harbour.
ex_zh: 1773年，殖民者被拒绝进入载有茶叶的船只，于是他们伪装起来，将茶叶倒入波士顿港。

def_en: help or support (financial assistance, medical assistance)
def_zh: 帮助（经济援助，医疗援助）
quote_en: The elderly woman needed assistance carrying her groceries.
quote_zh: 那位老妇人需要帮助搬运杂货。
ex_en: In 1804, Lewis and Clark received vital assistance from a Shoshone woman named Sacagawea during their expedition across America.
ex_zh: 1804年，刘易斯和克拉克在穿越美洲的探险中得到了肖肖尼族女子萨卡加维亚的重要帮助。

def_en: a vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling (ride a bicycle, bicycle lane)
def_zh: 自行车（骑自行车，自行车道）
quote_en: She learned to ride a bicycle when she was six years old.
quote_zh: 她六岁时学会了骑自行车。
ex_en: The first bicycle, called the "velocipede" or "boneshaker", was invented in 1817 and had wooden wheels and no pedals.
ex_zh: 第一辆自行车被称为“脚踏车”或“震骨器”，于1817年发明，有木制车轮，没有踏板。

def_en: the decision that something planned will not happen (flight cancellation, last‑minute cancellation)
def_zh: 取消（航班取消，最后一刻取消）
quote_en: The cancellation of the field trip disappointed the whole class.
quote_zh: 实地考察的取消让全班都很失望。
ex_en: In 1914, a single assassination led to the cancellation of many European royal vacations and, more seriously, the start of World War I.
ex_zh: 1914年，一次暗杀导致许多欧洲皇室假期被取消，更严重的是，引发了第一次世界大战。

def_en: a large area where dead people are buried (military cemetery, old cemetery)
def_zh: 公墓（军人公墓，古老墓地）
quote_en: Every November, we visit the cemetery to remember our ancestors.
quote_zh: 每年十一月，我们去公墓缅怀祖先。
ex_en: Arlington National Cemetery in Virginia is a famous military cemetery where over 400,000 soldiers are buried.
ex_zh: 弗吉尼亚州的阿灵顿国家公墓是一个著名的军人公墓，安葬了超过40万名士兵。

def_en: a group of people chosen to do a particular job or make decisions (organising committee, planning committee)
def_zh: 委员会（组织委员会，规划委员会）
quote_en: The school committee decided to buy new computers for the library.
quote_zh: 学校委员会决定为图书馆购买新电脑。
ex_en: The Continental Congress appointed a committee of five, including Thomas Jefferson, to draft the Declaration of Independence in 1776.
ex_zh: 1776年，大陆会议任命了一个五人委员会，包括托马斯·杰斐逊，起草《独立宣言》。

def_en: to move back or away from something because you are afraid or disgusted (cringe in fear, make someone cringe)
def_zh: 畏缩（因恐惧而畏缩，使人畏缩）
quote_en: The loud screech of chalk on the board made me cringe.
quote_zh: 粉笔在黑板上刺耳的声音让我畏缩。
ex_en: In medieval times, people would cringe at the sight of a plague doctor, whose mask looked like a scary bird.
ex_zh: 中世纪时，人们一看到瘟疫医生就会畏缩，因为医生的面具看起来像可怕的鸟。

def_en: simple and basic (elementary level, elementary mistake); relating to the early years of school (elementary school)
def_zh: 基础的（基础水平，基本错误）；小学的（小学）
quote_en: Learning to add and subtract is an elementary part of maths.
quote_zh: 学习加减法是数学的基础部分。
ex_en: In 350 BCE, Aristotle wrote an elementary guide to logic called "Organon", which was used for over 1,500 years.
ex_zh: 公元前350年，亚里士多德写了一本基础的逻辑指南《工具论》，被使用了超过1500年。

def_en: no longer existing (extinct species, extinct volcano)
def_zh: 灭绝的（灭绝物种，死火山）
quote_en: Dinosaurs became extinct about 65 million years ago.
quote_zh: 恐龙在大约6500万年前灭绝了。
ex_en: The dodo, a bird from Mauritius, became extinct in the 17th century because of hunting and introduced animals.
ex_zh: 渡渡鸟是毛里求斯的一种鸟，由于捕猎和外来动物的引入，于十七世纪灭绝了。

def_en: to pause before doing something because you are uncertain or afraid (hesitate to speak, not hesitate to help)
def_zh: 犹豫（犹豫说话，毫不犹豫帮助）
quote_en: Don't hesitate to ask for help if you get stuck.
quote_zh: 如果遇到困难，不要犹豫，尽管求助。
ex_en: During the American Revolution, some soldiers hesitated before firing, but others did not, changing the course of battles.
ex_zh: 美国独立战争期间，一些士兵在开火前犹豫了，但另一些没有，从而改变了战局。

def_en: not working or being used (idle machine, idle hands); lazy (idle student, idle person)
def_zh: 闲置的（闲置机器，闲着的手）；懒惰的（懒惰学生，懒人）
quote_en: The old factory has stood idle for ten years.
quote_zh: 旧工厂已经闲置了十年。
ex_en: In the 1930s Great Depression, many factories stood idle and millions of workers had no jobs.
ex_zh: 二十世纪三十年代大萧条期间，许多工厂闲置，数百万工人失业。

def_en: famous for something bad (infamous criminal, infamous event)
def_zh: 臭名昭著的（臭名昭著的罪犯，臭名昭著的事件）
quote_en: The pirate Blackbeard was infamous for his cruelty.
quote_zh: 海盗黑胡子因其残忍而臭名昭著。
ex_en: In 1929, Al Capone, the infamous gangster, ordered the St. Valentine's Day Massacre in Chicago.
ex_zh: 1929年，臭名昭著的黑帮头目阿尔·卡彭策划了芝加哥的情人节大屠杀。

def_en: to behave badly (children misbehave, misbehave in class)
def_zh: 行为不端（孩子调皮，在课堂上捣乱）
quote_en: If you misbehave during the trip, you will miss the next school outing.
quote_zh: 如果你在旅行中行为不端，你将错过下一次学校郊游。
ex_en: In Victorian England, students who misbehaved were often forced to wear a dunce cap and sit in a corner.
ex_zh: 在维多利亚时代的英格兰，行为不端的学生常被强迫戴上笨蛋帽，坐在角落里。

def_en: two lines that are always the same distance apart and never meet (parallel lines, parallel roads); something very similar (draw a parallel, historical parallel)
def_zh: 平行的（平行线，平行道路）；相似之处（作比较，历史相似点）
quote_en: The train tracks run parallel to the river for several miles.
quote_zh: 铁轨与河流平行延伸了数英里。
ex_en: In 1825, the first modern railway opened in England, and a parallel development in the US was the Baltimore & Ohio Railroad in 1828.
ex_zh: 1825年，第一条现代铁路在英国开通，而在美国类似的发展是1828年的巴尔的摩和俄亥俄铁路。

def_en: a group of words taken from a text or speech (famous quotation, direct quotation)
def_zh: 引文（著名引文，直接引语）
quote_en: She began her essay with a quotation from Martin Luther King Jr.
quote_zh: 她以马丁·路德·金的一句引言开始了她的文章。
ex_en: "Life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness" is a famous quotation from the US Declaration of Independence.
ex_zh: “生命、自由和追求幸福”是美国《独立宣言》中的一句著名引文。

def_en: a detailed examination of something (careful analysis, data analysis)
def_zh: 分析（仔细分析，数据分析）
quote_en: The scientist's analysis of the water showed high levels of pollution.
quote_zh: 科学家对水样的分析显示出高污染水平。
ex_en: In 1859, Charles Darwin published his analysis of evolution in "On the Origin of Species", changing biology forever.
ex_zh: 1859年，查尔斯·达尔文在《物种起源》中发表了他对进化的分析，永远改变了生物学。

def_en: small pieces of coloured paper thrown at celebrations (throw confetti, paper confetti)
def_zh: 五彩纸屑（抛洒五彩纸屑，纸屑）
quote_en: At the New Year's party, everyone threw confetti into the air.
quote_zh: 在新年派对上，每个人都向空中抛洒五彩纸屑。
ex_en: The tradition of throwing confetti started in Italy during the Renaissance, but it was originally made of flowers and seeds.
ex_zh: 抛洒五彩纸屑的传统始于文艺复兴时期的意大利，但最初是用鲜花和种子做的。

def_en: a person who has done something wrong (main culprit, catch the culprit)
def_zh: 罪犯（主犯，抓住罪犯）
quote_en: The detective finally found the culprit who stole the painting.
quote_zh: 侦探终于找到了偷画的罪犯。
ex_en: In 1666, the Great Fire of London started in a bakery; the culprit was probably a spark from the baker's oven.
ex_zh: 1666年，伦敦大火始于一家面包店；罪魁祸首很可能是面包师烤箱里迸出的火星。

def_en: to make someone believe something that is not true (deceive someone, try to deceive)
def_zh: 欺骗（欺骗某人，试图欺骗）
quote_en: He tried to deceive his parents by hiding the broken vase.
quote_zh: 他试图把打碎的花瓶藏起来欺骗父母。
ex_en: In the Trojan War, the Greeks used a wooden horse to deceive the Trojans into opening their gates.
ex_zh: 在特洛伊战争中，希腊人用木马计欺骗特洛伊人打开了城门。

def_en: the study of how money, goods, and services are produced and used (home economics, economics class)
def_zh: 经济学（家政学，经济学课）
quote_en: In economics, we learned about supply and demand.
quote_zh: 在经济学课上，我们学习了供求关系。
ex_en: Adam Smith, often called the father of economics, wrote "The Wealth of Nations" in 1776, the same year as the American Revolution.
ex_zh: 亚当·斯密常被称为经济学之父，于1776年（也是美国独立战争那年）写了《国富论》。

def_en: extremely interesting (fascinating story, fascinating discovery)
def_zh: 迷人的（迷人的故事，迷人的发现）
quote_en: The documentary about deep‑sea creatures was absolutely fascinating.
quote_zh: 关于深海生物的那部纪录片非常迷人。
ex_en: The discovery of the tomb of Tutankhamun in 1922 was a fascinating event that revealed incredible treasures.
ex_zh: 1922年图坦卡蒙墓的发现是一个令人着迷的事件，揭示了令人难以置信的宝藏。

def_en: forming the base or core of something (fundamental right, fundamental principle)
def_zh: 基本的（基本权利，基本原则）
quote_en: Learning to read is a fundamental skill for all other subjects.
quote_zh: 学习阅读是所有其他学科的基本技能。
ex_en: The fundamental idea of democracy is that government should be based on the consent of the governed.
ex_zh: 民主的基本理念是政府应基于被统治者的同意。

def_en: at once; without delay (act immediately, respond immediately)
def_zh: 立即（立即行动，立即回应）
quote_en: The fire alarm rang, so we immediately left the building.
quote_zh: 火警响了，我们立即离开了大楼。
ex_en: When the Japanese attacked Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941, the US immediately declared war the next day.
ex_zh: 1941年12月7日日本偷袭珍珠港后，美国次日立即宣战。

def_en: something that blocks your way or makes progress difficult (overcome an obstacle, major obstacle)
def_zh: 障碍（克服障碍，主要障碍）
quote_en: The biggest obstacle to finishing the project was lack of time.
quote_zh: 完成项目最大的障碍是时间不足。
ex_en: In 1805, the Lewis and Clark expedition faced the Rocky Mountains as a huge obstacle, but with Native American help they crossed it.
ex_zh: 1805年，刘易斯和克拉克探险队面临落基山脉这一巨大障碍，但在美洲原住民的帮助下成功穿越。

def_en: to make someone unable to move part or all of their body (paralyze a limb, fear paralyzes); to make something unable to function (paralyze traffic, paralyze the economy)
def_zh: 使瘫痪（使肢体瘫痪，恐惧使人僵住）；使无法运转（使交通瘫痪，使经济瘫痪）
quote_en: The snake's venom can paralyze a small animal within minutes.
quote_zh: 蛇的毒液能在几分钟内使小动物瘫痪。
ex_en: In 2010, a volcanic eruption in Iceland paralyzed air travel across Europe for several weeks.
ex_zh: 2010年，冰岛的一次火山喷发使整个欧洲的航空交通瘫痪了数周。

def_en: a basic truth or rule that guides behaviour or reasoning (moral principle, scientific principle)
def_zh: 原则（道德原则，科学原理）
quote_en: The principle of honesty guided his actions throughout life.
quote_zh: 诚实的原则指导了他一生的行动。
ex_en: Archimedes' principle states that a floating object displaces its own weight in water – he discovered this while taking a bath!
ex_zh: 阿基米德原理指出，漂浮物体排开的水量等于其自身重量——他是在洗澡时发现这一点的。

def_en: having the necessary skills or experience for a job (highly qualified, qualified teacher)
def_zh: 合格的（高度合格，合格教师）
quote_en: She is well qualified for the position because she studied engineering.
quote_zh: 她很适合这个职位，因为她学过工程学。
ex_en: In 1893, New Zealand became the first country where women were qualified to vote in national elections.
ex_zh: 1893年，新西兰成为第一个妇女有资格在全国选举中投票的国家。

def_en: fair and sensible (reasonable price, reasonable request); fairly good but not excellent (reasonable condition, reasonable quality)
def_zh: 合理的（合理价格，合理要求）；尚可的（状况尚可，质量尚可）
quote_en: The shop offered a reasonable refund for the damaged goods.
quote_zh: 商店为损坏的商品提供了合理的退款。
ex_en: The Magna Carta (1215) established the reasonable principle that even the king must follow the law.
ex_zh: 1215年的《大宪章》确立了即使是国王也必须遵守法律的合理原则。

def_en: showing an intention to harm or cause trouble (threatening letter, threatening behaviour)
def_zh: 威胁的（恐吓信，威胁行为）
quote_en: The dark clouds looked threatening, so we brought the laundry inside.
quote_zh: 乌云看起来很吓人，所以我们把衣服收进来了。
ex_en: In 1962, the Cuban Missile Crisis brought the world a threatening nuclear standoff between the US and the Soviet Union.
ex_zh: 1962年，古巴导弹危机使世界面临美国和苏联之间威胁性的核对峙。

def_en: worth a lot of money or very useful (valuable jewel, valuable advice)
def_zh: 宝贵的（贵重珠宝，宝贵建议）
quote_en: My grandmother gave me a valuable ring that belonged to her mother.
quote_zh: 我祖母给了我一个属于她母亲的贵重戒指。
ex_en: The Rosetta Stone was so valuable that French and British soldiers fought over it during the Napoleonic Wars.
ex_zh: 罗塞塔石碑非常宝贵，以至于法国和英国士兵在拿破仑战争期间为争夺它而战斗。

def_en: a small, flat, sweet cake (chocolate biscuit, digestive biscuit); a soft bread roll (biscuit and gravy)
def_zh: 饼干（巧克力饼干，消化饼干）；软面包卷（饼干配肉汁）
quote_en: I like to dip my biscuit into a cup of tea.
quote_zh: 我喜欢把饼干蘸在茶杯里。
ex_en: The word "biscuit" comes from Latin "bis coctus" meaning "twice baked" – a method used by Roman soldiers to make bread last longer.
ex_zh: “biscuit”一词来自拉丁语“bis coctus”，意为“两次烘烤”——罗马士兵用来延长面包保质期的方法。

def_en: an adult who accompanies and supervises young people (school chaperone, dance chaperone)
def_zh: 监护人（学校监护人，舞会监护人）
quote_en: Two parents volunteered as chaperones for the museum trip.
quote_zh: 两位家长自愿担任博物馆之旅的监护人。
ex_en: In the 19th century, a young woman could not go out in public without a chaperone, usually an older female relative.
ex_zh: 十九世纪，年轻女性没有监护人（通常是年长的女性亲戚）不能出现在公共场合。

def_en: to get or be given something (receive a gift, receive an award); to welcome a guest (receive visitors, warmly receive)
def_zh: 收到（收到礼物，获奖）；接待（接待访客，热情接待）
quote_en: I was happy to receive a letter from my pen pal.
quote_zh: 我很高兴收到笔友的来信。
ex_en: In 1863, President Lincoln received the Gettysburg Address draft on the train, writing most of it during the journey.
ex_zh: 1863年，林肯总统在火车上收到葛底斯堡演说的草稿，大部分内容是在旅途中写的。

def_en: a set of instructions for cooking a dish (secret recipe, family recipe)
def_zh: 食谱（秘方，家传食谱）
quote_en: This recipe calls for three eggs and two cups of flour.
quote_zh: 这个食谱需要三个鸡蛋和两杯面粉。
ex_en: The oldest known written recipe is for beer, found on a Sumerian clay tablet from around 1800 BCE.
ex_zh: 已知最古老的书面食谱是啤酒配方，发现于约公元前1800年的苏美尔泥板上。

def_en: to tell someone officially that they have done something wrong (sharply reprimand, reprimand a student)
def_zh: 训斥（严厉训斥，训斥学生）
quote_en: The teacher had to reprimand the class for being too noisy.
quote_zh: 老师不得不因为班级太吵而训斥他们。
ex_en: In 1773, when colonists refused to pay the tea tax, King George III reprimanded them by passing the Coercive Acts.
ex_zh: 1773年，当殖民者拒绝缴纳茶叶税时，国王乔治三世通过《强制法案》来训斥他们。

def_en: a place where you buy and eat a meal (Italian restaurant, fast‑food restaurant)
def_zh: 餐厅（意大利餐厅，快餐店）
quote_en: We celebrated my birthday at a nice restaurant downtown.
quote_zh: 我们在市中心一家不错的餐厅庆祝了我的生日。
ex_en: The first modern restaurant opened in Paris in 1765, serving "restorative" soups – that's where the word comes from.
ex_zh: 第一家现代餐厅于1765年在巴黎开业，供应“恢复性”汤——这就是这个词的来源。

def_en: a set of circumstances at a particular moment (difficult situation, win‑win situation)
def_zh: 情况（困难情况，双赢局面）
quote_en: The situation in the classroom was chaotic until the teacher returned.
quote_zh: 老师回来前，教室里的情况一片混乱。
ex_en: In 1940, the situation for British soldiers at Dunkirk was desperate, but hundreds of civilian boats rescued them.
ex_zh: 1940年，敦刻尔克英军的情况十分危急，但数百艘民用船只救出了他们。

def_en: a dirty mark or stain (ink smudge, grease smudge)
def_zh: 污迹（墨水污迹，油渍）
quote_en: There was a smudge of jam on his shirt after breakfast.
quote_zh: 早餐后他的衬衫上有一块果酱污迹。
ex_en: In medieval manuscripts, scribes often left smudges because quill pens could easily blot ink.
ex_zh: 在中世纪手抄本中，抄写员常留下墨迹，因为羽毛笔很容易弄脏。

def_en: a collection of numerical facts or data (official statistics, crime statistics)
def_zh: 统计数字（官方统计，犯罪统计）
quote_en: The statistics show that more people ride bikes to work than last year.
quote_zh: 统计数字显示，今年骑自行车上班的人比去年多。
ex_en: Florence Nightingale used statistics to prove that better hospital hygiene saved lives, inventing a new type of chart.
ex_zh: 弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔用统计数字证明更好的医院卫生能挽救生命，并发明了一种新式图表。

def_en: a person or thing used in place of another (substitute teacher, sugar substitute); to use something instead of another (substitute for butter)
def_zh: 替代品（代课老师，代糖）；替代（替代黄油）
quote_en: The substitute teacher didn't know our classroom rules.
quote_zh: 代课老师不知道我们的课堂规则。
ex_en: During World War II, people had to substitute margarine for butter and powdered eggs for fresh eggs.
ex_zh: 二战期间，人们不得不用人造黄油代替黄油，用蛋粉代替鲜鸡蛋。

def_en: a person who watches over workers or activities (shift supervisor, construction supervisor)
def_zh: 监督员（轮班主管，建筑监理）
quote_en: The lunchroom supervisor makes sure everyone cleans up after eating.
quote_zh: 午餐室的监督员确保每个人吃完后打扫干净。
ex_en: In the 1900s, factory supervisors used stopwatches to time workers, a method introduced by scientific management.
ex_zh: 二十世纪，工厂监督员用秒表给工人计时，这是科学管理引入的方法。

def_en: going from place to place (traveling circus, traveling salesman); the act of travelling (traveling abroad, long‑distance traveling)
def_zh: 旅行的（巡回马戏团，旅行推销员）；旅行（出国旅行，长途旅行）
quote_en: Traveling to new places helps you learn about different cultures.
quote_zh: 去新的地方旅行有助于了解不同文化。
ex_en: In the 13th century, Marco Polo spent 24 years traveling from Italy to China and back, writing a famous book about his adventures.
ex_zh: 十三世纪，马可·波罗花了24年从意大利旅行到中国并返回，写了一本关于他冒险的名著。

def_en: pointing straight up and down; perpendicular to the horizon (vertical line, vertical climb)
def_zh: 垂直的（垂直线，垂直爬升）
quote_en: The vertical cliff was too steep to climb without ropes.
quote_zh: 垂直的悬崖太陡，没有绳索无法攀登。
ex_en: The Eiffel Tower, built in 1889, has a vertical height of 300 metres – it was the world's tallest man‑made structure for 41 years.
ex_zh: 埃菲尔铁塔建于1889年，垂直高度300米——保持了41年世界最高人造建筑的纪录。

def_en: to form a mental picture of something (visualize success, visualize a scene)
def_zh: 想象（想象成功，想象场景）
quote_en: Close your eyes and visualize a peaceful beach.
quote_zh: 闭上眼睛，想象一个宁静的海滩。
ex_en: Before the Apollo 11 moon landing, astronauts practiced for months, visualizing every step of the mission.
ex_zh: 在阿波罗11号登月前，宇航员练习了数月，想象任务的每一步。

def_en: a wild, natural area not used by people (dense wilderness, Arctic wilderness)
def_zh: 荒野（茂密荒野，北极荒野）
quote_en: The hikers got lost in the wilderness for three days.
quote_zh: 徒步旅行者在荒野中迷路了三天。
ex_en: In 1804, Lewis and Clark explored the vast American wilderness, encountering grizzly bears, buffalo, and unknown plants.
ex_zh: 1804年，刘易斯和克拉克探索了广阔的美国荒野，遇到了灰熊、野牛和未知植物。

def_en: the date on which an important event happened in a previous year (wedding anniversary, 50th anniversary)
def_zh: 周年纪念日（结婚纪念日，50周年纪念）
quote_en: My parents celebrated their 20th wedding anniversary with a party.
quote_zh: 我父母办了一个派对庆祝他们的20周年结婚纪念日。
ex_en: In 1976, the United States celebrated the 200th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with tall ships and fireworks.
ex_zh: 1976年，美国用高桅帆船和烟火庆祝了《独立宣言》200周年。

def_en: a series of planned actions to achieve a goal (election campaign, advertising campaign)
def_zh: 活动；运动（竞选活动，广告宣传活动）
quote_en: The students launched a campaign to reduce plastic waste in the cafeteria.
quote_zh: 学生们发起了一项减少食堂塑料垃圾的活动。
ex_en: In 1776, George Washington led a military campaign across the Delaware River, surprising British troops on Christmas night.
ex_zh: 1776年，乔治·华盛顿领导了一场穿越特拉华河的军事行动，在圣诞夜突袭了英军。

def_en: very successful and respected (distinguished scientist, distinguished career)
def_zh: 杰出的（杰出科学家，杰出事业）
quote_en: The school invited a distinguished author to speak at assembly.
quote_zh: 学校邀请了一位杰出作家在集会上演讲。
ex_en: Marie Curie was a distinguished scientist who won Nobel Prizes in both Physics and Chemistry.
ex_zh: 玛丽·居里是一位杰出的科学家，获得了诺贝尔物理学奖和化学奖。

def_en: to give special importance to something (emphasize the point, strongly emphasize)
def_zh: 强调（强调这一点，强烈强调）
quote_en: The coach emphasized the importance of teamwork before the match.
quote_zh: 教练在比赛前强调了团队合作的重要性。
ex_en: In his "I Have a Dream" speech, Martin Luther King Jr. emphasized the need for racial equality by repeating the phrase.
ex_zh: 在“我有一个梦想”演讲中，马丁·路德·金通过重复该短语强调了种族平等的必要性。

def_en: to annoy or trouble someone repeatedly (harass workers, harass a neighbour)
def_zh: 骚扰（骚扰工人，骚扰邻居）
quote_en: It is against school rules to harass another student.
quote_zh: 骚扰其他学生是违反校规的。
ex_en: In 1964, the US Civil Rights Act made it illegal to harass someone based on their race or gender.
ex_zh: 1964年，美国《民权法案》规定基于种族或性别骚扰他人是非法的。

def_en: something that cannot happen or be done (physical impossibility, sheer impossibility)
def_zh: 不可能的事（物理上不可能，完全不可能）
quote_en: Winning the lottery is an impossibility if you don't buy a ticket.
quote_zh: 不买票的话中彩票是不可能的事。
ex_en: Before 1903, human flight was considered an impossibility, but the Wright Brothers proved everyone wrong.
ex_zh: 1903年之前，人类飞行被认为是不可能的事，但莱特兄弟证明了所有人都错了。

def_en: the confidence and spirits of a person or group (high morale, boost morale)
def_zh: 士气（高涨的士气，提振士气）
quote_en: The team's morale was high after winning three games in a row.
quote_zh: 连赢三场比赛后，球队士气高涨。
ex_en: During World War II, the British government used posters with the slogan "Keep Calm and Carry On" to boost public morale.
ex_zh: 二战期间，英国政府使用印有“保持冷静，继续前进”的海报来提振公众士气。

def_en: a person walking along a road or in a city (pedestrian crossing, pedestrian zone)
def_zh: 行人（人行横道，步行区）
quote_en: The pedestrian looked both ways before crossing the street.
quote_zh: 那位行人过马路前左右看了看。
ex_en: In ancient Rome, pedestrians had to share narrow streets with chariots – stepping into the mud was common.
ex_zh: 在古罗马，行人不得不与战车共用狭窄的街道——踩进泥里是常有的事。

def_en: water that falls from clouds to the ground as rain, snow, sleet, or hail (annual precipitation, heavy precipitation)
def_zh: 降水（年降水量，强降水）
quote_en: The forecast said precipitation was likely, so we took umbrellas.
quote_zh: 天气预报说可能有降水，所以我们带了雨伞。
ex_en: The Atacama Desert in South America has almost no precipitation – some areas have not seen rain for 400 years.
ex_zh: 南美洲的阿塔卡马沙漠几乎没有降水——有些地区已经400年没下过雨了。

def_en: the quality of being honest and truthful (genuine sincerity, speak with sincerity)
def_zh: 真诚（真正的真诚，真诚地说）
quote_en: I could tell from the sincerity in his voice that he meant what he said.
quote_zh: 我从他声音中的真诚能听出他说的是真心话。
ex_en: Abraham Lincoln was known for his sincerity, earning the nickname "Honest Abe" long before he became president.
ex_zh: 亚伯拉罕·林肯以真诚著称，早在成为总统之前就赢得了“诚实的亚伯”的绰号。

def_en: having doubts or not easily convinced (remain skeptical, skeptical of claims)
def_zh: 怀疑的（保持怀疑，对说法表示怀疑）
quote_en: I was skeptical about the magic trick until I saw it with my own eyes.
quote_zh: 在亲眼看到之前，我对那个魔术持怀疑态度。
ex_en: When Galileo claimed that the Earth moved around the Sun, many people were skeptical and refused to believe him.
ex_zh: 当伽利略声称地球围绕太阳旋转时，许多人持怀疑态度并拒绝相信他。

def_en: a competition with many players or teams (chess tournament, tennis tournament)
def_zh: 锦标赛（国际象棋锦标赛，网球锦标赛）
quote_en: Our school's basketball team won the regional tournament.
quote_zh: 我们学校的篮球队赢得了地区锦标赛。
ex_en: In medieval Europe, knights competed in jousting tournaments to show their skill and win prizes.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的欧洲，骑士们参加长矛比武锦标赛来展示技能并赢得奖品。

def_en: different from each other; of several kinds (various reasons, various colours)
def_zh: 各种各样的（各种原因，各种颜色）
quote_en: The museum had various exhibits, from dinosaurs to space rockets.
quote_zh: 博物馆有各种各样的展览，从恐龙到太空火箭。
ex_en: The Silk Road allowed various cultures – Chinese, Persian, Indian, Roman – to exchange goods and ideas.
ex_zh: 丝绸之路使各种文化——中国、波斯、印度、罗马——得以交换商品和思想。

def_en: a machine that carries people or goods from place to place (motor vehicle, electric vehicle)
def_zh: 车辆（机动车，电动车）
quote_en: A bus is a vehicle that can carry up to 70 passengers.
quote_zh: 公交车是一种能载多达70名乘客的车辆。
ex_en: In 1886, Karl Benz built the first true automobile – a three‑wheeled vehicle powered by a gasoline engine.
ex_zh: 1886年，卡尔·本茨制造了第一辆真正的汽车——一种由汽油发动机驱动的三轮车辆。

def_en: a long journey by sea or in space (ocean voyage, maiden voyage)
def_zh: 航行（远洋航行，首航）
quote_en: The family went on a voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.
quote_zh: 一家人进行了一次横渡大西洋的航行。
ex_en: In 1492, Christopher Columbus’s first voyage across the Atlantic took about 10 weeks to reach the Caribbean.
ex_zh: 1492年，克里斯托弗·哥伦布的第一次大西洋航行花了大约10周到达加勒比海。

def_en: made by humans, not natural (artificial light, artificial sweetener)
def_zh: 人造的（人造光，人造甜味剂）
quote_en: The park has an artificial lake that was dug by workers.
quote_zh: 那个公园有一个人工湖，是工人们挖的。
ex_en: The first artificial satellite, Sputnik 1, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1957, starting the Space Age.
ex_zh: 第一颗人造卫星斯普特尼克1号于1957年由苏联发射，开启了太空时代。

def_en: a small booklet or pamphlet with information (travel brochure, information brochure)
def_zh: 小册子（旅行手册，信息手册）
quote_en: We picked up a brochure about the national park at the visitor centre.
quote_zh: 我们在游客中心拿了一本关于国家公园的小册子。
ex_en: In the 1800s, railway companies printed brochures to encourage people to take scenic train journeys.
ex_zh: 十九世纪，铁路公司印制小册子鼓励人们乘坐风景优美的火车旅行。

def_en: to show something publicly (exhibit art, exhibit behaviour); a public display (museum exhibit, travelling exhibit)
def_zh: 展览（展览艺术品，表现行为）；展品（博物馆展品，巡回展览）
quote_en: The science museum will exhibit the dinosaur skeleton next month.
quote_zh: 科学博物馆下个月将展出那具恐龙骨架。
ex_en: In 1851, the Great Exhibition in London exhibited inventions from around the world, including the first voting machine.
ex_zh: 1851年，伦敦万国工业博览会展览了来自世界各地的发明，包括第一台投票机。

def_en: very unusual or remarkable (extraordinary talent, extraordinary event)
def_zh: 非凡的（非凡天赋，非凡事件）
quote_en: The rescue of the trapped miners was an extraordinary effort.
quote_zh: 营救被困矿工是一项非凡的努力。
ex_en: The discovery of penicillin in 1928 by Alexander Fleming was an extraordinary accident that saved millions of lives.
ex_zh: 1928年亚历山大·弗莱明发现青霉素是一个非凡的意外，拯救了数百万人的生命。

def_en: to invent a false story or fact (fabricate a lie, fabricate evidence); to make or build something (fabricate parts, fabricate a machine)
def_zh: 编造（编造谎言，伪造证据）；制造（制造零件，制造机器）
quote_en: The witness fabricated his story and was caught lying.
quote_zh: 目击者编造了他的故事，被抓住了撒谎。
ex_en: In 1726, a woman named Mary Toft fabricated a hoax, claiming she gave birth to rabbits, fooling many doctors.
ex_zh: 1726年，一位名叫玛丽·托夫特的女子编造了一个骗局，声称她生了兔子，欺骗了许多医生。

def_en: to lose or give up something as a penalty (forfeit a game, forfeit a right)
def_zh: 丧失（比赛弃权，丧失权利）
quote_en: If you cheat, you may forfeit your place on the team.
quote_zh: 如果你作弊，你可能失去在队中的位置。
ex_en: In 1919, eight baseball players were banned for life and had to forfeit their World Series medals for throwing games.
ex_zh: 1919年，八名棒球运动员因打假球被终身禁赛，并不得不交出他们的世界大赛奖牌。

def_en: a part of a city where a particular group lives, often in poor conditions (Jewish ghetto, urban ghetto)
def_zh: 贫民区（犹太区，城市贫民区）
quote_en: The museum tells the story of the Warsaw ghetto during World War II.
quote_zh: 博物馆讲述了二战期间华沙犹太区的故事。
ex_en: In Venice, the word "ghetto" was first used in 1516 for an area where Jews were forced to live.
ex_zh: 在威尼斯，“ghetto”一词最早于1516年用于指犹太人被迫居住的区域。

def_en: too attractive or strong to resist (irresistible urge, irresistible charm)
def_zh: 不可抗拒的（不可抗拒的冲动，不可抗拒的魅力）
quote_en: The smell of fresh cookies was irresistible.
quote_zh: 新鲜饼干的香味不可抗拒。
ex_en: In Greek mythology, the Sirens' song was said to be irresistible, luring sailors to crash on the rocks.
ex_zh: 在希腊神话中，塞壬的歌声据说不可抗拒，引诱水手撞上礁石。

def_en: clear enough to read (legible handwriting, barely legible)
def_zh: 清晰的（清晰的笔迹，勉强可读）
quote_en: Please write your name in legible capital letters.
quote_zh: 请用清晰的大写字母写你的名字。
ex_en: The oldest legible writing in the world is on Sumerian clay tablets from about 3400 BCE.
ex_zh: 世界上最古老的清晰文字写在约公元前3400年的苏美尔泥板上。

def_en: the process of closing a business and selling its assets (company liquidation, forced liquidation)
def_zh: 清算（公司清算，强制清算）
quote_en: The store went into liquidation and sold everything at half price.
quote_zh: 那家商店进入清算，所有商品半价出售。
ex_en: During the Great Depression, thousands of banks went into liquidation because people withdrew all their money.
ex_zh: 大萧条期间，数千家银行因人们取走所有存款而进入清算。

def_en: a small thin book with paper covers, containing information (political pamphlet, instruction pamphlet)
def_zh: 小册子（政治小册子，说明书）
quote_en: The doctor gave me a pamphlet about healthy eating.
quote_zh: 医生给了我一册关于健康饮食的小册子。
ex_en: In 1776, Thomas Paine wrote a 47‑page pamphlet called "Common Sense" that convinced many Americans to support independence.
ex_zh: 1776年，托马斯·潘恩写了一本47页的小册子《常识》，说服了许多美国人支持独立。

def_en: feeling or showing sudden, uncontrollable fear (get panicky, panicky reaction)
def_zh: 恐慌的（变得恐慌，恐慌反应）
quote_en: People became panicky when the fire alarm went off.
quote_zh: 火警响起时人们变得恐慌。
ex_en: In 1938, a radio drama of "The War of the Worlds" made some listeners panicky, as they thought Martians were really invading.
ex_zh: 1938年，广播剧《世界大战》让一些听众感到恐慌，以为火星人真的入侵了。

def_en: the scientific study of the mind and behaviour (child psychology, educational psychology)
def_zh: 心理学（儿童心理学，教育心理学）
quote_en: In psychology class, we learned why people dream.
quote_zh: 在心理学课上，我们学习了人为什么会做梦。
ex_en: Sigmund Freud, known as the father of psychoanalysis, wrote "The Interpretation of Dreams" in 1899, changing psychology forever.
ex_zh: 西格蒙德·弗洛伊德被称为精神分析之父，于1899年写了《梦的解析》，永远改变了心理学。

def_en: a figure of speech comparing two things using "like" or "as" (use a simile, famous simile)
def_zh: 明喻（使用明喻，著名明喻）
quote_en: "As brave as a lion" is a common simile.
quote_zh: “像狮子一样勇敢”是一个常见的明喻。
ex_en: Homer, the ancient Greek poet, used epic similes in the Iliad, comparing warriors to lions or raging fires.
ex_zh: 古希腊诗人荷马在《伊利亚特》中使用了史诗般的明喻，将战士比作狮子或烈火。

def_en: clearly defined or identified (specific instructions, specific reason)
def_zh: 具体的（具体指示，具体原因）
quote_en: The teacher gave specific examples of how to cite sources.
quote_zh: 老师给出了如何引用来源的具体例子。
ex_en: In 1859, Charles Darwin gave a specific explanation of how species evolve by natural selection.
ex_zh: 1859年，查尔斯·达尔文对物种如何通过自然选择进化给出了具体的解释。

def_en: a wide street, often with trees (tree‑lined boulevard, famous boulevard)
def_zh: 林荫大道（绿树成荫的大道，著名大道）
quote_en: We walked along the boulevard past beautiful old houses.
quote_zh: 我们沿着林荫大道走过美丽的旧房子。
ex_en: The Champs‑Élysées in Paris is one of the most famous boulevards in the world, built in the 17th century.
ex_zh: 巴黎的香榭丽舍大街是世界上最著名的林荫大道之一，建于十七世纪。

def_en: a bunch of flowers arranged attractively (wedding bouquet, bouquet of roses)
def_zh: 花束（新娘花束，玫瑰花束）
quote_en: He gave his mother a bouquet of lilies for her birthday.
quote_zh: 他给了母亲一束百合花作为生日礼物。
ex_en: In Victorian England, people used the language of flowers – each bouquet sent a secret message.
ex_zh: 在维多利亚时代的英格兰，人们使用花的语言——每个花束都传达一个秘密信息。

def_en: as a result; therefore (consequently leading to, and consequently)
def_zh: 因此（从而导致，因而）
quote_en: The storm damaged power lines; consequently, school was cancelled.
quote_zh: 风暴损坏了电线，因此学校停课了。
ex_en: In 1348, the Black Death killed many European workers; consequently, surviving workers could demand higher wages.
ex_zh: 1348年，黑死病杀死了许多欧洲工人；因此，幸存的工人可以要求更高的工资。

def_en: a person who is related to someone from the past (direct descendant, distant descendant)
def_zh: 后代（直系后代，远亲后代）
quote_en: She is a descendant of the Mayflower pilgrims who arrived in 1620.
quote_zh: 她是1620年到达的五月花号清教徒的后代。
ex_en: Queen Elizabeth II was a descendant of Queen Victoria, who ruled England for 63 years.
ex_zh: 伊丽莎白二世女王是维多利亚女王的后代，维多利亚女王统治英国63年。

def_en: obviously wrong or offensive (flagrant violation, flagrant disregard)
def_zh: 公然的（公然违反，公然漠视）
quote_en: Cheating on the exam was a flagrant violation of school rules.
quote_zh: 考试作弊是对校规的公然违反。
ex_en: In 1773, the colonists saw the tea tax as a flagrant abuse of power by the British Parliament.
ex_zh: 1773年，殖民者将茶叶税视为英国议会对权力的公然滥用。

def_en: a complaint about something unfair (file a grievance, legitimate grievance)
def_zh: 不满（提出申诉，合理不满）
quote_en: The workers listed their grievances about low pay and long hours.
quote_zh: 工人们列出了他们对低工资和长时间工作的不满。
ex_en: The American Declaration of Independence lists 27 grievances against King George III, including unfair taxes.
ex_zh: 美国《独立宣言》列举了对国王乔治三世的27项不满，包括不公平的税收。

def_en: widespread destruction or chaos (wreak havoc, cause havoc)
def_zh: 浩劫（造成严重破坏，引起混乱）
quote_en: The windstorm wreaked havoc, knocking down trees and power lines.
quote_zh: 风暴造成了严重破坏，吹倒了树木和电线。
ex_en: In 1666, the Great Fire of London caused such havoc that it destroyed 13,000 houses and 87 churches.
ex_zh: 1666年，伦敦大火造成了巨大的浩劫，摧毁了13,000座房屋和87座教堂。

def_en: practices that keep you clean and healthy (personal hygiene, dental hygiene)
def_zh: 卫生（个人卫生，口腔卫生）
quote_en: Good hygiene includes washing your hands before eating.
quote_zh: 良好的卫生包括饭前洗手。
ex_en: In the 1840s, Ignaz Semmelweis discovered that doctors washing hands drastically reduced deaths, but his ideas were rejected at first.
ex_zh: 十九世纪四十年代，伊格纳兹·塞麦尔维斯发现医生洗手能大大降低死亡率，但他的想法最初被拒绝了。

def_en: a clever or skillful move (military maneuver, political maneuver); to move carefully (maneuver through traffic)
def_zh: 策略（军事演习，政治策略）；巧妙地移动（在车流中穿行）
quote_en: The driver made a sharp maneuver to avoid the dog.
quote_zh: 司机做了一个急转弯动作以避开那条狗。
ex_en: In 1944, the Allies used a huge military maneuver called D-Day to land in Normandy and free Europe.
ex_zh: 1944年，盟军使用了一次名为“D日”的巨大军事行动，在诺曼底登陆并解放了欧洲。

def_en: a person or thing causing trouble or annoyance (public nuisance, minor nuisance)
def_zh: 麻烦事（妨害公共利益，小麻烦）
quote_en: The loud music from next door was a constant nuisance.
quote_zh: 隔壁传来的吵闹音乐一直是件烦心事。
ex_en: In 1907, a nuisance law in San Francisco made it illegal to leave a horse on the street for too long.
ex_zh: 1907年，旧金山的一项妨害法规规定将马长时间留在街上是违法的。

def_en: a large bird with a long tail, often hunted for food (ring‑necked pheasant, pheasant hunting)
def_zh: 野鸡（环颈雉，打野鸡）
quote_en: The farmer saw a colourful pheasant in the field.
quote_zh: 农民在田里看到一只色彩斑斓的野鸡。
ex_en: In medieval England, pheasants were introduced from Asia and became a favourite dish at royal banquets.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的英格兰，野鸡从亚洲引入，成为皇家宴会上最受欢迎的菜肴之一。

def_en: a lack of something; when there is not enough (water scarcity, food scarcity)
def_zh: 短缺（水资源短缺，粮食短缺）
quote_en: During the drought, there was a scarcity of fresh vegetables.
quote_zh: 干旱期间，新鲜蔬菜短缺。
ex_en: During World War II, scarcity of rubber led to the invention of synthetic rubber in the United States.
ex_zh: 二战期间，橡胶短缺导致美国发明了合成橡胶。

def_en: serious and formal (solemn ceremony, solemn promise)
def_zh: 庄严的（庄严的仪式，庄严的承诺）
quote_en: The soldiers stood at solemn attention during the memorial service.
quote_zh: 士兵们在追悼会期间庄严地立正。
ex_en: In 1863, Abraham Lincoln gave a solemn speech at Gettysburg to honour soldiers who died in the Civil War.
ex_zh: 1863年，亚伯拉罕·林肯在葛底斯堡发表庄严演说，以纪念在内战中阵亡的士兵。

def_en: an object kept as a reminder of a place or event (tourist souvenir, souvenir shop)
def_zh: 纪念品（旅游纪念品，纪念品商店）
quote_en: I bought a small Eiffel Tower souvenir from Paris.
quote_zh: 我从巴黎买了一个小小的埃菲尔铁塔纪念品。
ex_en: In the 19th century, wealthy Europeans collected souvenir coins from ancient Roman ruins during their Grand Tour.
ex_zh: 十九世纪，富有的欧洲人在他们的壮游期间收集古罗马遗址的纪念币。

def_en: in a way that uses a lot of energy and strength (exercise vigorously, argue vigorously)
def_zh: 精力充沛地（精力充沛地锻炼，激烈争辩）
quote_en: She brushed her teeth vigorously for two full minutes.
quote_zh: 她用力地刷了两分钟牙。
ex_en: In 1912, Theodore Roosevelt campaigned vigorously for president, even after being shot in the chest during a speech.
ex_zh: 1912年，西奥多·罗斯福精力充沛地竞选总统，即使他在一次演讲中被枪击胸部后仍坚持。
